summary: Package's only function downloads and executes an opaque Windows binary from an attacker-controlled GitHub repo
details: fastercode 0.1.1 has no documentation, no author, no description, and a single exported function `run()` (re-exported from `__init__.py`). On Windows, that function downloads `BackgroundSyncService.exe` from `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/manhhungdev0603/kl.py/refs/heads/main/BackgroundSyncService.exe` — a personal user account, mutable `main` branch, and a repo named `kl.py` consistent with a keylogger — into `%PROGRAMDATA%\BackgroundSyncService\` and launches it via `subprocess.Popen([...], shell=True)` (fastercode/core.py lines 8-22). There is no hash check, no signature verification, and no version pin; the bytes that execute are whatever the GitHub user has at the URL at fetch time. The deceptive `BackgroundSyncService` filename, the absence of any legitimate functionality in the package, and the empty PKG-INFO metadata indicate this package exists solely to drop and run an attacker-controlled Windows executable on caller machines.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
